tldr: Countly is an open-source, self-hostable product analytics platform with strong mobile SDK support. Event taxonomy and user properties port; you trade managed scale for control and lower TCO.
why_switch: Mobile-first SDKs for iOS, Android, Unity
export_method: Use Amplitude's Export API to pull raw events for the time range you need, and the warehouse-native sync if it is configured. Save cohorts and chart definitions via the dashboard. Document the full event taxonomy so you can re-implement it cleanly in Countly. Be sure to capture both `user_id` and `device_id` per event so identity stitching can be reproduced. Confirm the end date of any annual event commitment via your account settings.
import_method: Deploy Countly via Docker, Kubernetes, or the official packaged installer; or use Countly Cloud. Create an app in the Countly dashboard and grab the App Key + Server URL. Install the matching Countly SDK in your application and replicate the events you previously sent to Amplitude — `logEvent` becomes `Countly.recordEvent`. For historical events, use Countly's bulk events endpoint to backfill with original timestamps so retention/funnel charts stay accurate. Rebuild key cohorts and dashboards in Countly's UI. Run both SDKs in parallel for a week, compare DAU and event counts, then remove Amplitude.
gotchas: Amplitude annual event commit does not pro-rate
Self-hosted Countly adds infra and ops cost — budget realistically
Historical event timestamps must be preserved on bulk import
Identity model differs — verify user/device merge produces same DAU
License is non-standard (NOASSERTION on repo) — review before commercial use

email.subject: Data export and wind-down — Amplitude
email.body:
Hi Amplitude team,

We plan to migrate to a self-hosted analytics platform over the next 14 days. Could you help with:
1. Confirming the recommended path to export full historical events, cohorts, and chart definitions via the Export API or warehouse sync.
2. Sharing the end date of our current annual event-volume commitment so we can plan cutover.
3. Confirming the data retention/purge timeline after cancellation.

We will run instrumentation in parallel for a short overlap period and then cancel. Please flag anything that requires your team to action.

Thanks,
Your customer
